Section Title: Background > References
Content:
["How the Ilitches used 'dereliction by design' to get their new Detroit arena"](https://web.archive.org/web/20210108040156/https://www.metrotimes.com/news-hits/archives/2017/09/12/how-the-ilitches-used-dereliction-by-design-to-get-their-new-detroit-arena) . *Detroit Metro Times* . Archived from [the original](https://www.metrotimes.com/news-hits/archives/2017/09/12/how-the-ilitches-used-dereliction-by-design-to-get-their-new-detroit-arena) on January 8, 2021 . Retrieved January 5, 2021 . **^** Gross, Allie (May 23, 2018). ["Blighted Ilitch-owned buildings near Little Caesars Arena go unticketed"](https://www.freep.com/story/news/local/michigan/detroit/2018/05/23/blight-tickets-ilitch-cass-avenue-detroit/623246002/) . *Detroit Free Press* . Retrieved January 5, 2021 .

Section Title: Background
Content:
[edit ]
Mike and Marian Ilitch started Little Caesars in 1959. A complementary business, Blue Line Foodservice, was soon started to supply ingredients and other products to the growing restaurant chain. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup>
In 1982, Ilitch entered into sports with the purchase of the struggling Red Wings. Ilitch, executing his long-term Detroit business conglomerate plan, also purchased Olympia Stadium Corporation that year. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> Olympia Stadium operated the Joe Louis and Cobo arenas . <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup>
The couple purchased and restored the downtown Fox Theatre in 1987. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> In 1988, they fielded an indoor football team, Detroit Drive , in the Arena Football League . <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> In 1992, Mike purchased the Detroit Tigers . <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> Purchasing the Tigers led him to sell the Drive in February 1994 given the leagues' overlapping schedules. <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup>

**Ilitch Holdings, Inc.** is an American holding company established in 1999 to provide all companies owned by Mike and Marian Ilitch with professional and technical services. Its privately held businesses include Little Caesars Pizza , the National Hockey League (NHL) Detroit Red Wings , the Major League Baseball (MLB) Detroit Tigers , Olympia Entertainment , Olympia Development, Olympia Parking, Blue Line Foodservice Distribution, Champion Foods, 313 Presents , the Little Caesars Pizza Kit Fundraising Program, Hockeytown Cafe, and a variety of venues within these entities. Ilitch Holdings subsidiaries manage Detroit's Fox Theatre") , City Theatre") , Comerica Park , Pine Knob Music Theatre , Michigan Lottery Amphitheater, Meadow Brook Amphitheater, and Little Caesars Arena , which replaced Joe Louis Arena after closing in July 2017. <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>
Ilitch Holdings, Inc. is headquartered in the Fox Theatre Building in Detroit , Michigan.
